chapter_context.summary: Rehoboam rejects the advice of older counselors, sparking a rebellion of the ten northern tribes under Jeroboam; Jeroboam sets up golden calf temples at Dan and Bethel to secure his power.

outline.text: The Assembly of Israel Requests Rehoboam to Lighten Their Yoke
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Assembly of Israel Requests Rehoboam to Lighten Their Yoke (12:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Rehoboam Rejects the Elders' Advice; Listens to the Young Men
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rehoboam Rejects the Elders' Advice; Listens to the Young Men (12:6-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: The Ten Northern Tribes Revolt: 'To your tents, O Israel!'
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Ten Northern Tribes Revolt: 'To your tents, O Israel!' (12:16-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Jeroboam Anointed King Over the Northern Tribes
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeroboam Anointed King Over the Northern Tribes (12:20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Rehoboam Prevented from Civil War by the Prophet Shemaiah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rehoboam Prevented from Civil War by the Prophet Shemaiah (12:21-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Jeroboam Establishes Golden Calves at Bethel and Dan
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeroboam Establishes Golden Calves at Bethel and Dan (12:25-33), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 12, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
